meta_title: For Founders: SaaS pricing to Solve no coding experience (Beginner Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no coding experience. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: SaaS Pricing Strategy for Non-Coders: A Founder's Guide
content.seo_description: Master SaaS pricing without writing code. Learn to build automated revenue models using Antigravity and Claude Code in this step-by-step beginner tutorial.
content.answer_block: You don't need to code to launch a profitable SaaS. By leveraging Antigravity for rapid UI assembly and Claude Code for logic orchestration, you can implement dynamic pricing models that convert. Focus on value-based tiers, automate your checkout flow, and let AI handle the heavy lifting of backend integration.
content.intro:
The graveyard of SaaS startups is filled with founders who spent months wrestling with syntax instead of talking to customers. You have a vision, but the technical barrier feels like an impenetrable wall. The reality? Coding is no longer a prerequisite for building high-margin software; it is merely an execution detail that modern AI tools have commoditized.

Today, we dismantle that wall. By using Antigravity to architect your frontend and Claude Code to drive the logic, you shift from being a 'coder' to being a 'product architect.' We are going to deploy a production-ready pricing engine that turns visitors into recurring revenue, all without writing a single line of raw code.

steps.title: Define Value-Based Pricing Tiers
steps.description: Before building, you must map your features to user personas. We define the 'Free', 'Pro', and 'Enterprise' tiers based on usage limits rather than just feature lists.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a responsive SaaS pricing table component with three cards. Include toggles for monthly/yearly billing and clear call-to-action buttons for each tier.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate a pricing schema JSON that maps feature access to user tier IDs'

steps.title: Architect the Checkout Flow
steps.description: The checkout experience is where revenue happens. We use Antigravity to create a clean, high-conversion modal that connects to Stripe.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Build a clean, minimalist checkout modal that accepts user email and displays the selected plan price, with a placeholder for a Stripe payment button.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create a checkout validation function that verifies the selected plan against the pricing schema'

steps.title: Automate Logic with Claude Code
steps.description: We need to ensure that when a user pays, their account is updated. We use Claude Code to wire up the logic between the UI and your backend.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a state management script that updates the user's access level upon successful form submission.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create a webhook handler structure that updates the user database record based on a successful payment payload'

steps.title: Implement Dynamic Upsell Triggers
steps.description: Maximize LTV by triggering a 'Pro' upgrade prompt when a user reaches 80% of their free tier usage limit.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a toast notification component that appears when a user hits a usage threshold, inviting them to upgrade.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'write a monitoring function that compares current user usage metrics against tier limits and returns an upgrade boolean'

steps.title: Deploy and Validate
steps.description: We push the build to production to verify that the pricing logic is correctly gating features.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor the current project structure for a production-ready deployment, ensuring all environment variables are correctly mapped.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'run a final integration test on the payment flow to ensure tier access is correctly toggled'

pro_tips: Never charge based on cost-plus; always charge based on the time saved or revenue generated for your user.
Use 'anchoring' by placing your most expensive plan in the middle to make the middle-tier look like the best value.
Offer a 'Founder's Lifetime' tier early on to generate quick cash flow for initial development costs.
Monitor 'Drop-off' points in your checkout flow using simple session recording tools to see where users hesitate.
Always include a 'Contact Us' link for Enterprise plans; enterprise buyers prefer human negotiation over self-serve checkouts.
content.next_steps: Once your pricing is live, focus on driving your first 100 users to the landing page to test conversion rates. Iterate on the copy based on user feedback.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools and the strategy. Now, you need the momentum. Don't let another day pass without launching your revenue model. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
